sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers photochemotherapy (i.e., psoralens and ultraviolet A [PUVA]), excimer laser (e.g., XTRAC, PhotoMedex, Radnor, PA; EX-308, Ra Medical Systems, Inc., Carlsbad, CA), or narrow- band ultraviolet B (NB-UVB) medically necessary for the treatment of vitiligo when the member has had an inadequate response to any of the following: Topical tacrolimus; or Topical and systemic corticosteroids; or Topical ruxolitinib (Opzelura).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers continued PUVA or narrow-band UVB therapy not medically necessary unless there is significant follicular pigmentation after 6 months of therapy (8 to 10 treatments per month). II.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Aetna considers the following procedures experimental, investigational, or unproven because the effectiveness of these approaches has not been established: A. ApaI, BsmI, catalase (389C>T), cystathionine B synthase (CBS), cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen (CTLA)-4+49A/G, human leukocyte antigen-A, NLRP1, methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR), protein tyrosine phosphatase, non-receptor type 22 +1858C→T, and TNF- alpha-308G/A gene polymorphisms testing for early detection of vitiligo B. Measurement of interleukin-17 (IL-17) levels for the management of vitiligo C. Use of digital and analog image analysis systems (e.g., 2D digital imaging analysis and 3D photography) for surface calculation of vitiligo lesions D. The following interventions for the treatment of vitiligo (not an all-inclusive list): Apremilast (alone or combined with NB-UVB) Autologous mini-punching grafting Blister roof grafting (suction epidermal blister grafting) (e.g., CelluTome Epidermal Harvesting System) Capecitabine CAR-T-cell therapy Carbon dioxide fractional laser Chimeric monoclonal antibody to CD20 (e.g., rituximab) Combined baricitinib and narrow-band ultraviolet B (NB-UVB) phototherapy Combined fractional CO2 laser and NB-UVB phototherapy Fire needle therapy Glutathione Home phototherapy Interleukins Intradermal mesotherapy (injections of bio-revitalizant NCTF135) Melagenine α-melanocyte stimulating hormone (e.g., afamelanotide) Melanocyte transplantation/cultured and non-cultured cellular melanocyte keratinocyte transfer Microneedle-based drug delivery approaches Neovir (an intramuscular immunomodulatory agent, composed of sodium oxodihydroacridinylacetate) Platelet-rich plasma Prostaglandins (e.g., bimatoprost, latanoprost, and prostaglandin E2) Split thickness skin grafting Tars Topical minoxidil Topical phenytoin gel Topical pseudocatalase Tumor necrosis factor-alpha agents (e.g., adalimumab, etanercept, and infliximab) Vitamin D analogs (e.g., calcitriol and paricalcitol). III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Cosmetic Aetna considers treatments for vitiligo cosmetic if they do not affect the underlying condition and do not result in improved protection against skin cancer; specifically micropigmentation (tattooing) and depigmentation (with monobenzylether of hydroquinone/monobenzone) are considered cosmetic. IV.
sentences.label: CLAIM

depigmented lesions.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The 3 prevailing theories of the pathogenesis of vitiligo include an immune hypothesis, a neural-mediated hypothesis, and a “self-destruct” hypothesis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These 3, plus newer hypotheses suggesting that vitiligo may be due to a melanocyte growth factor deficiency or to an abnormal melatonin receptor on melanocytes, have not been definitively proven, and it is likely that the loss of epidermal and follicular melanocytes in vitiligo may be the result of several different pathogenic mechanisms.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Psoralen photochemotherapy (psoralen and ultraviolet light A or PUVA) is appropriate for properly selected patients with vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The treatment involves taking oral psoralen or applying it topically followed by carefully timed exposure to UVA.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Repigmentation may begin after 15 to 25 treatments.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Response to PUVA is unlikely to occur if follicular pigmentation has not appeared after 3 months of PUVA therapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Dark-skinned patients respond better than fair skin patients do; the latter are unlikely to benefit from PUVA unless there is marked disfigurement.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Most patients who respond do not develop new areas of pigment loss; furthermore, maintenance with PUVA therapy is not necessary.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients must apply the cream (e.g., triamcinolone 0.1 %, desonide 0.05 %) once-daily to the white patches on their skin for at least 3 to 4 months before seeing any results.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Oral mini-pulse therapy with 5 mg betamethasone/dexamethasone may stop the progression and induce spontaneous repigmentation in some vitiligo patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Narrow-band UVB is typically administered 2 to 3 times per week for several months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, there is a lack of evidence regarding the safety and effectiveness of home narrow-band UVB phototherapy for the treatment of vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a randomized controlled study, Ada et al (2005) concluded that narrow-band UVB phototherapy is effective in treating vitiligo, and the addition of topical calcipotriol does not improve treatment outcome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a double-blind randomized study, Yones et al (2007) compared the effectiveness of oral PUVA with that of narrowband-UVB (NB-UVB) phototherapy in patients with non-segmental vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that 389C>T may not contribute to vitiligo susceptibility.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, larger primary studies with the consideration of gene-environment and gene-gene interactions are still needed to further evaluate the interaction of CAT gene polymorphism with vitiligo susceptibility.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a meta-analysis, Li and colleagues (2015) evaluated the association between 2 common polymorphisms (ApaI and BsmI) in the VDR gene and the susceptibility to vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Human Leukocyte Antigen-A Polymorphism Testing Li and colleagues (2016) evaluated the association between vitiligo and human leukocyte antigen- (HLA-) A. Methods.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Overall, three alleles (HLA- A⁎02, A⁎33, and Aw⁎31) were significantly associated with increased risk of vitiligo, two (HLA-A⁎09 and Aw⁎19) were associated with decreased risk, and the remaining 28 were unassociated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that they identified a potential genetic relationship in the Caucasian population between the NLRP1 rs12150220 polymorphism and a decreased susceptibility to autoimmune diseases, especially autoimmune Addison's disease, T1DM, or SLE.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Blister Roof Grafting Janowska and colleagues (2016) stated that vitiligo is a multi-factorial acquired dermatosis characterized by achromic or hypochromic macules and by the absence of functioning melanocytes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a pilot study, the authors stated that this system was seen to be a safe and effective means of harvesting epidermal micrografts containing melanocytes for use in patients with stable vitiligo unresponsive to standard therapies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cai and associates (2016) stated that epidermal grafting has several advantages over full-thickness or split- thickness grafts in the treatment of complex non-healing wounds.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the automated SBEG harvesting device was a safe and effective option for treating complex non-healing wounds in multi-morbid patients who may be poor surgical candidates.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These investigators stated that in dealing with complex wounds, patient selection is an integral component of a successful outcome; patients with co-morbidities not suitable for the operating room, wound healing issues, and compliance concerns are poor candidates for traditional skin grafts.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The autologous epidermal grafts effectively circumvent these problems and present an attractive alternative.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The creation of an automated SBEG harvesting technique further simplified the procedure and minimized post-operative complications.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that although this study has shown success in a variety of patients, identification of the ideal patient population may be of interest in follow-up studies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Krishna and colleagues (2016) stated that vitiligo is a common pigmentary disorder of the skin with a great amount of social stigma attached to it.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although various medical modalities are available for the treatment of stable vitiligo, surgical modality remains the treatment of choice for stable and localized vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The surgical options range from simple punch grafting to the recent epidermal harvesting methods using a negative pressure unit.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers noted that new automated epidermal harvesting system now commercially available involves a tool that applies both heat and suction concurrently to normal skin to induce epidermal micrografts; it serves as a safe, quick and cost-effective method without anesthesia, with a very minimal downtime for healing and requires an optimal expertise.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The duration of re- pigmentation appeared to be faster and more uniform compared to other procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that more controlled studies are needed to prove the effectiveness of negative pressure epidermal harvesting in patients with stable vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Dillon and colleagues (2017) stated that latanoprost (LT) is a prostaglandin F2-alpha analog that can induce skin pigmentation, a side effect discovered via its use in the treatment of glaucoma.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings indicated that topical phenytoin of low concentrations may have beneficial effects through immunomodulatory activity by affecting CD4 and CD8 counts and subsequently the ratio between them.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They stated that further studies are recommended to combine phenytoin with other anti-vitiligo agents such as local corticosteroids or phototherapy to clarify if it could potentiate their effects.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors described how the association of the daily use of topical 2 % minoxidil with alternate day PUVA, was able to induce local hyperthricosis and marker re-pigmentation in 2 vitiligo patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Unfortunately, no other studies about minoxidil in vitiligo have been conducted and some clinical reports described controversial results, such as the appearance of leucoderma after the use of the drug.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Topical cream containing pseudocatalase has been proposed as a therapeutic tool for vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The drug acts by reducing the free radicals and improving the catalase action. Generally, it is applied twice- daily.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Better results appeared to be achieved when pseudocatalase is associated to sol-therapy, UVA or nb-UVB.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Gianfaldoni and colleagues (2018b) noted that Neovir is an intramuscular Immunomodulatory agent, composed of sodium oxo – dihydro – acridinyl - acetate (ODHAA).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: It is usually used to normalize impaired immune system functions under various conditions, such as viral infections, immunodeficiency, oncological diseases and multiple sclerosis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vitiligo progression was assessed in 1, 3, 6 and 12 months after treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results of the preliminary study were excellent: sodium oxodihydroacridinylacetate showed high efficiency in achieving long-term stabilization of non-segmental vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Carbon Dioxide Fractional Laser Chen and colleagues (2018) noted that tacrolimus is a conventional medication for the treatment of vitiligo, but the effect of a single medication is limited.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers examined the effects, adverse responses, and re-pigmentation results of the joint treatment of vitiligo by carbon dioxide (CO2) fractional laser together with tacrolimus.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the treatment of vitiligo by CO2 fractional laser together with tacrolimus was effective and was most suitable for patients in the progression stage.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Chiu and associates (2018) stated that the treatment of stable non-segmental vitiligo is often challenging, which new therapies are being searched.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Multiple clinical trials have proposed the benefits and safety of using fractional CO2 laser as an adjunct therapy to conventional treatments.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers examined the safety and efficacy of fractional CO2 laser as a combination therapy to conventional treatments in patients with stable non-segmental vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: the authors concluded that the findings of this meta-analysis showed that using fractional CO2 laser in combination with conventional treatments was safe and efficient, and may be considered as an adjunct therapeutic option for patients with refractive non-segmental vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The studies highlight the crucial role of IL-17 in the onset and progression of the disease, and its synergistic action with IL-2, IL-6 and IL-33.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings showed the growing need for new therapies targeting vitiligo and further research on the role of ILs as a treatment is needed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study suggested a possible role of altered Hcy and vitamin B12 levels in precipitation and progression of vitiligo in genetically susceptible individuals.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Interestingly, MTHFR 1298 A > C polymorphism was found to be associated with autoimmune vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: El Tahlawi and colleagues (2020) noted that an elevated Hcy level has been described in vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) and cystathionine B synthase (CBS) are major determinants of the homocysteine metabolism.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers examined serum Hcy levels in vitiligo patients as well as the association between MTHFR (C677T, A1298C) and CBS gene polymorphisms and susceptibility to vitiligo in a sample of those populations; Hcy levels were estimated by radioimmunoassay while MTHFR (C677T, A1298C) and CBS gene polymorphisms were detected by the PCR-RFLP technique in 100 vitiligo patients and 80 healthy controls.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Hcy level was significantly higher in vitiligo patients than controls (p = 0.000).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Significant differences in the genotype and allele distributions of single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) of the MTHFR (C677T, A1298C) with the mutant genotypes were more common in the controls than patients (p = 0.001, 0.029, respectively); CBS gene mutant genotypes and alleles were more common in vitiligo patients than controls (p = 0.002).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that CBS and MTHFR gene polymorphisms may play a major role in the genetic susceptibility to vitiligo.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Chang and associates (2020) stated that MTHFR is an important enzyme that converts 5,10- methylenetetrahydrofolate into 5-methylenetetrahydrofolate, which provides the methyl group to convert Hcy to methionine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Two common MTHFR gene polymorphisms, C677T (rs1801133) and A1298C (rs1801131), are associated with decreased MTHFR enzyme activity, and several studies have reported the involvement of these polymorphisms in susceptibility to diseases, including autoimmune diseases.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vitiligo is a common cutaneous hypo-pigmentation disease resulting from the loss of functional melanocytes due to autoreactive CD8+ T cells or oxidative stress in genetically predisposed individuals.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There was a significant difference between CMT and CMT + phototherapy in stable vitiligo (p 0.05).
sentences.label: PREMISE
